item: Move the archived previews off the public 'analysis' folder in R2
dependencies: REF-05 slices 1-3 (private preview write + reader); full-audio capture landed
key_locations: apps/web/src/lib/server/preview-bucket-migration.ts (the copy/verify/delete core); apps/web/src/lib/server/orpc/admin-migrations.ts + packages/contracts/src/orpc/admin-migrations.ts (operator-tier migrate_preview_archive op); apps/cli/src/commands/preview-migration.ts (CLI client, `fluncle admin migrations preview-archive`)
open_decision: None — dry-run-first; operator runs copy → verify → --delete-public → purge the found.fluncle.com Cloudflare cache
analysis: BUILT (REF-05 slice 4). The migration ships as the operator-tier migrate_preview_archive op plus the `fluncle admin migrations preview-archive` CLI — Worker-side off the existing VIDEOS + SOURCE_AUDIO bindings (needs no R2 creds). Two explicitly-separate phases (never in one pass). PHASE A copies each legacy analysis/previews/<logId>/<sha256>.<ext> object into the private fluncle-source-audio bucket at <logId>/preview.<ext> — sha256-verified on read AND on private read-back — then rewrites preview_archive_key. PHASE B (--delete-public) removes the now-orphaned public object only after confirming the private copy is present; the old public key is reconstructed from the private bytes' hash. Dry-run is the DEFAULT; --execute mutates. Idempotent + batched + resumable. REMAINING: the operator runs copy → verify → --delete-public then PURGES the Cloudflare cache for the found.fluncle.com hostname (deleting the R2 origin object alone leaves the edge serving the cached copy — the leak stays live). Depends on slices 1-3 (private preview write + agent-tier preview-audio reader); this slice merges LAST.

item: Audit + review R2 settings/config across all buckets
backlog_status: Audited 2026-07-08 — posture SOUND; TLS finding RESOLVED (operator); 2 optional backup hardenings remain (operator policy calls)
key_locations: wrangler r2 bucket cors/lifecycle/info/domain/dev-url/lock/sippy (LIVE reads work via current OAuth auth); apps/web/r2-cors.json; wrangler.jsonc bindings; fluncle buckets = fluncle-videos / fluncle-backups / fluncle-source-audio
analysis: AUDIT 2026-07-08 (wrangler live read-only, all 3 fluncle-* buckets). POSTURE SOUND overall: public r2.dev dev-url DISABLED on all three (no accidental public exposure); a multipart-abort-after-7d lifecycle on all three; object-lock + sippy off everywhere; no event-notification rules. Per bucket — fluncle-videos (world-served at found.fluncle.com, 778 obj / 9.5 GB, binding VIDEOS): live CORS == apps/web/r2-cors.json EXACTLY (public GET/HEAD rule + fluncle.com/localhost PUT/POST/DELETE with ETag exposed). ONE FINDING (RESOLVED 2026-07-08): the found.fluncle.com custom domain floored min-TLS at 1.0 (deprecated) → operator bumped it to 1.2 (verified live: min_tls_version 1.2, cert re-provisioning). fluncle-source-audio (full-song archive, 61 obj / 283 MB, binding SOURCE_AUDIO): CORRECTLY PRIVATE — no CORS, no custom domain, dev-url disabled — the copyright-sensitive full-song bucket is verified NOT publicly reachable (matches audio-source-policy internal-only). fluncle-backups (box-only token, 8 obj / 790 kB, intentionally UNBOUND in wrangler = correct): no CORS / no domain / not public ✓. OPTIONAL hardenings on backups: (a) a retention lifecycle to expire stale backups, (b) an object-lock/WORM retention rule for ransomware-tamper protection. Every remediation is an operator/dashboard-gated mutation; the audit itself was read-only.

analysis: RFC FINAL 2026-07-07 (Forge: 3 research threads + taste + 3-role adversarial panel). Approach: STOP DISCARDING the number the pipeline already emits (sweeps already parse claude -p usage/total_cost_usd + OpenRouter usage + Cartesia char count and throw them away).
